steps.title: Substitute u = (x+5)² to identify the key variable
steps.content:
The function $p(x) = a((x + 5)^2 - b)((x + 5)^2 - c)$ has $(x+5)^2$ appearing in both factors. Let $u = (x+5)^2$. Then:
$$p(x) = a(u - b)(u - c)$$
Crucially, $p(x)$ depends only on the value of $u = (x+5)^2$, not on $x$ itself. This means different values of $x$ can produce the same output if they share the same $(x+5)^2$ value.

steps.title: Find u values for all four points
steps.content:
Calculate $(x+5)^2$ at each point:

  • $x = -6$: $u = (-6+5)^2 = (-1)^2 = 1$, and we know $p(-6) = 30$
  • $x = 0$: $u = (0+5)^2 = 25$, and we know $p(0) = 342$
  • $x = -10$: $u = (-10+5)^2 = (-5)^2 = 25$
  • $x = -4$: $u = (-4+5)^2 = 1^2 = 1$

The symmetry is now clear: $x = -6$ and $x = -4$ both yield $u = 1$, while $x = 0$ and $x = -10$ both yield $u = 25$.

steps.title: Apply symmetry to find the sum
steps.content:
Since $p(x)$ depends only on $u = (x+5)^2$, values of $x$ with the same $u$ produce identical outputs:
$$p(-4) = p(-6) = 30 \quad \text{both have } u = 1$$
$$p(-10) = p(0) = 342 \quad \text{both have } u = 25$$
Therefore:
$$p(-10) + p(-4) = 342 + 30 = 372$$
